currentMainHead.subject: 🐀 Rat Cleanup: Consolidate gameplay, accessibility, verification, and docs (#57, #60, #61, #62, #63, #65, #85, #86, #87, #88, #94, #95) (#96)

conclusion: The active queue is small enough for one integration batch, but only after planned pruning and split work.
#97 is close to mergeable as-is, including its intentional Scurry journal update.
#98 should not be merged as-is; rats-the-video-game/index.html plus the Palette-Rat journal update belong in the batch, but the root helper scripts and screenshots do not.
#14 remains deferred outside this automated run and should keep its manual-follow-up label.

conflictResolutionNotes: Integrate the squeak mechanic first so the shared tutorial/help text settles before modal UX refinements land.
Treat .Jules/*.md journal files as intentional logs when they accompany related behaviour changes; do not treat them as stray review noise.
Keep #14 out of this batch entirely because it is already deferred and DIRTY against current main.

manual-follow-up; exclude from automated execute scope

requiredFixesBeforeMerge.fix: Use an outcome-style PR title if a direct representative PR is reopened later.
Port rats-the-video-game/index.html plus .Jules/palette-rat.md; leave the root helper scripts and generated screenshots out of the repo history for this batch.
Replace the square-bracket title style before direct merge or closure messaging so the queue remains outcome-first and stylistically consistent.
Keep deferred until a separate manual review decides whether to refresh it onto current main or replace it with a smaller follow-up.

scopeCoherenceAndSplitPlan.rationale: The player-facing mechanic, help-text changes, and Scurry journal update belong together.
The UX improvement and Palette-Rat journal update are valid, but the rest is local scaffolding and generated output rather than reviewable product code.
The experiment is broader than the current gameplay/UI queue and is already labelled for manual handling.

Integrate #98 second as a scoped port after the noisy helper and screenshot files are excluded.
